subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Go Math Texas: Middle-of-Year Test

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Represent fractions with models / equal parts

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Measure liquid volume or weight: units & tools

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Tell time to the nearest minute — prerequisite

prompt: Isabella has 40 paper clips. She places the same number of paper clips in each of 8 envelopes. Which related fact shows the number of paper clips in each envelope?
explanation: 40 ÷ 8 = 5, and the related fact is 8 × 5 = 40, so each envelope holds 5.

prompt: Angel is drawing a sketch for a painting. Each unit square is 1 square inch. The grid is 8 by 6. Which equation can be used to find the area of the painting?

options.rationale: The grid is 8 by 6, not 8 by 5.

explanation: Area = length × width = 8 × 6 = 48 square inches.

prompt: Ava is gluing a ribbon around a picture frame with side lengths of 5 inches and 8 inches. How many inches of ribbon will Ava use?

options.rationale: 40 is the area (5 × 8), not the perimeter.
13 is just 5 + 8, one length and one width.

explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (5 + 8) = 26 inches.

prompt: Joseph colored part of a circle that is divided into fourths. He colored 3 of the 4 parts. What fraction of the circle did he color?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 120 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M60,60 L60.0,10.0 A50,50 0 0 1 110.0,60.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L110.0,60.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,110.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L60.0,110.0 A50,50 0 0 1 10.0,60.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L10.0,60.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,10.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figures.alt: circle in fourths with 3 parts shaded

options.rationale: More than one part is colored.
Three parts are colored, not two.

explanation: 3 of 4 equal parts are colored, so 3/4.

prompt: Mr. Ortiz earns $9 per hour working at a restaurant. How much does he earn in 5 hours?
explanation: Equal pay each hour: 9 × 5 = 45 dollars.

prompt: The number of students in Kimberly's school is 1,154. Which shows another way to write 1,154?
Which set of numbers contains all even numbers?
explanation: Even numbers end in 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8. All of 336, 484, 952 are even.

prompt: On a number line from 0 to 1 marked in eighths, which fraction names the point closest to 0?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="70.0" y1="33" x2="70.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="70.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1/8</text><line x1="110.0" y1="33" x2="110.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="110.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2/8</text><line x1="150.0" y1="33" x2="150.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3/8</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4/8</text><line x1="230.0" y1="33" x2="230.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="230.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5/8</text><line x1="270.0" y1="33" x2="270.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6/8</text><line x1="310.0" y1="33" x2="310.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="310.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7/8</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text></svg>

explanation: Among the choices, 4/8 is the smallest, so it is closest to 0.

prompt: There are 5 stands at a farmer's market. Each stand has 30 baskets of vegetables. How many baskets of vegetables are at the farmer's market?
explanation: Equal groups: 5 × 30 = 150 baskets.

prompt: Emma leaves for school at 7:50 A.M. She eats breakfast 30 minutes before she leaves and wakes up 15 minutes before she eats breakfast. What time does Emma wake up?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 140 140" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="70" cy="70" r="58" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="3"/><text x="92.0" y="36.9" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><text x="108.1" y="53.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2</text><text x="114.0" y="75.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3</text><text x="108.1" y="97.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4</text><text x="92.0" y="113.1" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5</text><text x="70.0" y="119.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6</text><text x="48.0" y="113.1" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7</text><text x="31.9" y="97.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">8</text><text x="26.0" y="75.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9</text><text x="31.9" y="53.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">10</text><text x="48.0" y="36.9" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">11</text><text x="70.0" y="31.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">12</text><line x1="70" y1="70" x2="35.4" y2="50.0" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="70" y1="70" x2="47.1" y2="86.1" stroke="black" stroke-width="3"/><circle cx="70" cy="70" r="3" fill="black"/></svg>

options.rationale: That is more than 45 minutes before 7:50.
That is only 15 minutes before she leaves.

prompt: Which fraction is represented by the sum 1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4?

options.rationale: Adding fourths keeps 4 on the bottom.

explanation: Three copies of 1/4 make 3/4.

prompt: Brianna earns $20 babysitting every week. She saves $5 each week. How much will she save in 6 weeks?

options.rationale: That is not $5 saved for 6 weeks.
That is only 5 weeks of saving.

explanation: She saves $5 each week: 5 × 6 = 30 dollars.

prompt: Davis wants to buy 8 songs online. Each song costs $2. He has $10 saved and will borrow the rest from his mother. He will pay her back $2 each week. How many weeks will it take to pay her back?
explanation: 8 songs × $2 = $16. He borrows 16 − 10 = $6. At $2 per week, 6 ÷ 2 = 3 weeks.

prompt: Alex has 48 photos of a trip in one album and 36 photos of the trip in another album. How many photos of his trip does he have in the two albums?
explanation: Add the two albums: 48 + 36 = 84 photos.

prompt: Caleb has 35 stamps. He places 5 stamps on each page of a book. How many pages does he use?
explanation: Divide into equal pages: 35 ÷ 5 = 7 pages.

prompt: Ian has 1 basket. There are 8 apples in the basket. How many apples does Ian have?
explanation: One basket holds all 8 apples.

prompt: Trinity has two $5 bills and three $1 bills in her pocket. How much money does Trinity have?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 150" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="10" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="43" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$5</text><rect x="84" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="117" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$5</text><rect x="158" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="191" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="232" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="265" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="10" y="54" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="43" y="76" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text></svg>

explanation: Two $5 bills = $10, three $1 bills = $3, total $13.

prompt: Which measurement unit should be used for the weight of a golf ball?

options.rationale: A golf ball is too light to measure best in pounds.
Liters measure liquid volume, not weight.

explanation: A small, light golf ball is best measured in ounces.

figures.alt: L-shaped pond on a grid, 26 unit squares

explanation: Count the unit squares inside the L-shaped outline: 26 squares, so the area is 26 square feet. [Counted from the scanned grid.]

prompt: Taya runs a 200-meter race. Len runs a 400-meter race. Sam runs 500 fewer meters than Taya and Len together. How many meters does Sam run?

options.rationale: That is the total before subtracting 500.

prompt: Francisco has 3 puppies. Each puppy has 3 tags on its collar. Which expression shows how many tags the puppies have altogether?

options.rationale: That is only two groups added.
That does not show equal groups.

explanation: 3 puppies × 3 tags = 3 × 3 = 9 tags.

prompt: Christina has 12 pictures. Which describes one array Christina could make using all 12 pictures?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 88 72" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="16" cy="16" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="32" cy="16" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="48" cy="16" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="64" cy="16" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="16" cy="32" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="32" cy="32" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="48" cy="32" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="64" cy="32" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="16" cy="48" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="32" cy="48" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="48" cy="48" r="4" fill="#333"/><circle cx="64" cy="48" r="4" fill="#333"/></svg>

explanation: 3 × 4 = 12, so 3 rows of 4 uses all 12 pictures.

prompt: Which tool would you use to measure the mass of a pineapple?

options.rationale: A measuring cup measures liquid volume.

explanation: Mass is measured with a balance (or scale).

prompt: Damian divides one wall of his room into 6 equal sections to paint. If he paints one section, what fraction of the wall does he paint?
explanation: One of 6 equal sections is 1/6 of the wall.

prompt: Molly has 36 stickers. She puts 9 stickers on each page in a sticker book. How many pages does she use?
explanation: Divide into equal pages: 36 ÷ 9 = 4 pages.

prompt: Jesse divides a game board into 4 equal sections. What fraction names one section of the game board?
explanation: One of 4 equal sections is 1/4.

prompt: Elijah has 40 pieces of paper. He takes 4 pieces. Then he hands 4 pieces to each classmate until they are gone. To how many classmates does Elijah give paper?

options.rationale: That forgets the 4 pieces he took.

explanation: After he takes 4: 40 − 4 = 36 pieces. At 4 per classmate: 36 ÷ 4 = 9 classmates.

prompt: The population of Mission, Texas, in 2011 was 79,368. Which digit in 79,368 is in the hundreds place?
explanation: From the right: 8 ones, 6 tens, 3 hundreds, 9 thousands, 7 ten-thousands. The hundreds digit is 3.

prompt: It costs $8 to rent a bicycle for one hour. How much will it cost for Josie and her 4 friends to each rent a bicycle for one hour?
explanation: Josie and 4 friends = 5 people: 5 × $8 = $40.

prompt: Sadie and Ruby had equal-size pears. Sadie ate 3/4 of her pear and Ruby ate 2/4 of her pear. Which statement is true?

options.text: Sadie and Ruby ate the same amount.
There is no way to determine who ate more.
options.rationale: Same-size pears and same denominator let us compare.

explanation: Same-size pears, same denominator: 3/4 > 2/4, so Sadie ate more.

prompt: Estella read for 1/2 hour. Using a number line marked in sixths, which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="83.3" y1="33" x2="83.3" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="83.3" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1/6</text><line x1="136.7" y1="33" x2="136.7" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="136.7" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2/6</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3/6</text><line x1="243.3" y1="33" x2="243.3" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="243.3" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4/6</text><line x1="296.7" y1="33" x2="296.7" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="296.7" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5/6</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><circle cx="190.0" cy="40" r="6" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: number line in sixths with 3/6 marked

explanation: 1/2 lines up with 3/6 on the number line.

prompt: Mariana has 3 bowls. Each bowl has 9 grapes. How many grapes does she have in all?
explanation: Equal groups: 3 × 9 = 27 grapes.

prompt: The populations of three cities are 12,578, 12,534, and 12,509. Which shows these numbers in order from greatest to least?

options.rationale: 12,578 is the greatest and should be first.

prompt: Brandon hiked 1/3 of a mile. Using a number line marked in thirds and sixths, which fraction is equivalent to 1/3?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="83.3" y1="33" x2="83.3" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="83.3" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1/6</text><line x1="136.7" y1="33" x2="136.7" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="136.7" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2/6</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3/6</text><line x1="243.3" y1="33" x2="243.3" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="243.3" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4/6</text><line x1="296.7" y1="33" x2="296.7" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="296.7" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5/6</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><circle cx="136.7" cy="40" r="6" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: number line in sixths with 2/6 marked

explanation: 1/3 lines up with 2/6 on the number line.

prompt: Edith has 87 animal stickers and 32 sports stickers. About how many stickers does she have in all?

options.rationale: Round each to the nearest ten first.

explanation: Round and add: 87 ≈ 90 and 32 ≈ 30, so about 90 + 30 = 120 stickers.

prompt: Six friends share 2 oranges equally. Each orange is divided into thirds. What fraction of an orange does each friend get?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 120 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M60,60 L60.0,10.0 A50,50 0 0 1 103.3,85.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L103.3,85.0 A50,50 0 0 1 16.7,85.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L16.7,85.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,10.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>

options.rationale: The oranges are split into thirds, not fourths.
Each friend gets less than half.
Each piece is a third of an orange.

explanation: 2 oranges = 6 thirds, shared among 6 friends = 1 third each.

prompt: Angela reads 36 pages. Ivan reads 45 pages. How many pages do Angela and Ivan read altogether?
explanation: Add the pages: 36 + 45 = 81.

prompt: Madeline plays 4 songs on the piano. Jonathan plays twice as many songs. Which expression represents the number of songs Jonathan plays?
explanation: "Twice as many as 4" = 4 × 2 = 8.

prompt: Marty has 3 cups that each have 6 marbles. Which fact in the same fact family helps you check how many marbles Marty has in all?

options.rationale: These numbers are not in the fact family of 3 × 6.

explanation: 3 × 6 = 18 marbles. The related division fact is 18 ÷ 6 = 3.

prompt: Which three-dimensional solid has exactly 1 vertex?
explanation: A cone has 1 vertex — the point at its top.

prompt: Mabel's mom biked 4,187 miles in one year. What is 4,187 rounded to the nearest thousand?

options.rationale: The hundreds digit is 1, so round down.

explanation: The hundreds digit is 1 (less than 5), so 4,187 rounds down to 4,000.

prompt: Mr. Carson has 158 red, 241 green, 197 yellow, and 207 orange pieces of paper. Of which color does he have the most?
explanation: Compare: 241 (green) is the greatest count.

prompt: Miranda gives money to a food bank. Which word describes this decision?

options.rationale: She is not keeping the money.

explanation: Donating money to a food bank is giving.

prompt: Aaliya drew an isosceles trapezoid, a right trapezoid, a rectangle, and a square. How many of these quadrilaterals are trapezoids?

options.rationale: The rectangle and square are not trapezoids.
The rectangle and square have two pairs of parallel sides.

explanation: A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides. The two trapezoids qualify; the rectangle and square do not. That is 2.

prompt: Keesha has 32 foam balls. She takes 4 balls to play a game. She places the rest in bags that each hold 4 balls. How many bags does she use?

options.rationale: That forgets the 4 balls she took.

explanation: After she takes 4: 32 − 4 = 28 balls. At 4 per bag: 28 ÷ 4 = 7 bags.

prompt: Helena drew a number line from 0 to 1 marked in sixths. Point Q is between 1/6 and 3/6. Which fraction names point Q?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="83.3" y1="33" x2="83.3" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="83.3" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1/6</text><line x1="136.7" y1="33" x2="136.7" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="136.7" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">Q</text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3/6</text><line x1="243.3" y1="33" x2="243.3" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="243.3" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4/6</text><line x1="296.7" y1="33" x2="296.7" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="296.7" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5/6</text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><circle cx="136.7" cy="40" r="6" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: number line in sixths with point Q at 2/6

explanation: Point Q is 2 sixths from 0, so it is 2/6.

prompt: Kyle has $12. He wants to save $18 to buy an art set. He spends $5 on a book. How much more money does he need to save to buy the art set?

options.rationale: He already has some money toward the set.
He does not need to save $30.
$7 is what he has left, not what he still needs.

explanation: After the book he has 12 − 5 = $7. He needs $18, so 18 − 7 = $11 more.

prompt: A store has fewer of some granola bar flavors and charges more for them. Prices: Chocolate $3, Peanut butter $4, Coconut $6, Almond $4. Of which flavor does the store have the most?

options.rationale: $6 is the highest price, so the fewest in stock.

explanation: Lower price means more in stock. Chocolate is cheapest ($3), so the store has the most chocolate bars.

prompt: Use the Baskets of Berries Picked dot plot (5→3 people, 6→5, 7→2, 8→1, 9→3). How many more people picked 6 baskets than picked 7 or 8 baskets combined?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 304 175" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="150" x2="264" y2="150" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="30" y1="145" x2="30" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5</text><circle cx="30" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="30" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="30" cy="104" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="86" y1="145" x2="86" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="86" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6</text><circle cx="86" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="104" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="88" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="72" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="142" y1="145" x2="142" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="142" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7</text><circle cx="142" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="142" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="198" y1="145" x2="198" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="198" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">8</text><circle cx="198" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="254" y1="145" x2="254" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="254" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9</text><circle cx="254" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="254" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="254" cy="104" r="7" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: dot plot of baskets of berries picked

options.rationale: That is the count for 6 alone.

explanation: 6 baskets = 5 people. 7 (2) + 8 (1) = 3. Difference = 5 − 3 = 2.

prompt: Use the Baskets of Berries Picked dot plot (5→3 people, 6→5, 7→2, 8→1, 9→3). What number of baskets was picked by the least number of people?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 304 175" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="20" y1="150" x2="264" y2="150" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="30" y1="145" x2="30" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5</text><circle cx="30" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="30" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="30" cy="104" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="86" y1="145" x2="86" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="86" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6</text><circle cx="86" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="104" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="88" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="86" cy="72" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="142" y1="145" x2="142" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="142" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7</text><circle cx="142" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="142" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="198" y1="145" x2="198" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="198" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">8</text><circle cx="198" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><line x1="254" y1="145" x2="254" y2="155" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="254" y="170" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9</text><circle cx="254" cy="136" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="254" cy="120" r="7" fill="black"/><circle cx="254" cy="104" r="7" fill="black"/></svg>
figures.alt: dot plot of baskets of berries picked

explanation: 8 baskets has only 1 dot — the fewest people.
